detail: Issue #394 claimed: status:available -> status:in-progress

npm install --ignore-scripts --workspace=core; added @huggingface/transformers ^4.2.0; pre-existing node-gyp Zoom-native build failure side-stepped
copied 5 files from predecessor rnd/diarization/src/ to core/src/services/diarization/; CJS adaptation (dropped import.meta.url / fileURLToPath); tsc shows no new errors
new TeamsAttributor with windowMatch + clusterVote + provisional cluster_id; 22 assertions pass
rewrote handleTeamsAudioData (caption no longer triggers flush); diarizer + attributor singletons wired into Teams pipeline init; no fallback
AudioWorklet lowpass NOT required — bot uses AudioContext({sampleRate:16000}), Chromium handles polyphase resample; note recorded
scripts/bake-diarization-models.js downloads pyannote+wespeaker into transformers.js cache; Dockerfile ts-builder runs it after npm run build; cache flows to runtime via existing node_modules COPY

eval:suite — recall=90.1% purity=95.7% collab=96.9% (realistic noise); pyannote-probe strict@200ms=87.9% vs wespeaker baseline 85.3% (+2.6pp); eval:score recall=90.4% purity=96.2% byte-match prior baseline; transcript=0% due to missing TRANSCRIPTION_API_TOKEN, not regression
docker build vexaai/vexa-bot:0.10.6.2-260530-2244 succeeded; bake step pyannote+wespeaker → /app/vexa-bot/core/node_modules/@huggingface/transformers/.cache (32M verified in runtime layer)
BLOCKED — preflight required interactive transcription-token prompt (no TTY) and stored token returned HTTP 403 (expired). Operator gate.
deploy/lite/Dockerfile.lite gains the same bake-diarization-models step after npm run build

bot+bake portion (steps 1-25/49) PASS; dashboard Next.js prebuild step 39/49 FAILED with module-not-found on route.ts files — pre-existing main-branch issue, out of pack scope (services/dashboard/ untouched). No fresh vexa-lite image produced.
pack-evidence-check.py --mode draft: PASS
pack-evidence-check.py --mode pr-ready: FAIL — missing operator-signed compose/human-eyeball.md, lite/human-eyeball.md, human/overall-functionality.md status:pass + pr.md (now created)
render-pr-body.py wrote .agents/packs/.../pr.md
review.md + code-review.md + tests/README.md + hardenloop/README.md + human/overall-functionality.md written; human verdict files all marked PENDING OPERATOR

BOT_DEV_MODE branch in entrypoint.sh + optional VEXA_BOT_DEV_SRC_MOUNT in runtime-api/profiles.yaml + empty-mount filter in api.py + dev/hot-dev.md + dev/hot-bot.sh; collapses edit-test wall time from ~10min image rebuild to seconds
vexaai/vexa-bot:0.10.6.2-260531-1211 bakes the hot-dev entrypoint
point Compose .env at local-prod transcription LB (http://host.docker.internal:8085) — reuses 4 healthy GPU workers vs spinning up an isolated stack
Docker subnet pool exhausted (5 active pack stacks); pre-created pack-msteams-diar-cutover-compose_vexa on explicit subnet 10.140.65.0/24 instead of touching other stacks
pack-msteams-override.yml drops TTS 127.0.0.1:8002 host port (conflicts with stitched 0.10.6.3 stack) + injects BOT_DEV_MODE/VEXA_BOT_DEV_SRC_MOUNT into runtime-api env

make all PASS (with override); 10 containers healthy; transcription validated against http://172.17.0.1:8085 (local-prod LB, 4 GPU workers, large-v3-turbo); ports 42300/42301/42304/42305/42306/42307/42313/42314 bound; dashboard 200, gateway 200, admin 200
runtime-api recreated with BOT_DEV_MODE=1 and host-source mount path; next bot spawn picks up host code via tsx (no rebuild)

hot-bot.sh probe + docker exec confirms: (1) entrypoint logs 'BOT_DEV_MODE=1 — meeting bot via tsx src/docker.ts', (2) host source mounted at /app/vexa-bot/core/src — docker.ts and index.ts byte sizes match host, diarization/ + teams-attributor.* present, (3) baked ONNX cache present at node_modules/@huggingface/transformers/.cache/onnx-community/, (4) no HF fetch, (5) no caption-flush regression. Per-speaker pipeline init (and diarizer.create) not exercised — that runs on real meeting join, gated on operator-provided Teams URL.
hot-bot.sh now only adds -it when stdin+stdout are both TTYs — works in both interactive and CI/headless contexts
upstream commit fa86a09 not in 0.10.6.2 base — bot's unified-callback.ts lacked X-Internal-Secret header → meeting-api 403. Ported the header mirror; bot joined past callback
hot-dev mount shadows src/; addInitScript looked for src/browser-utils.global.js which only exists in dist/. Added ../dist/ fallback path

tsx (esbuild) inserts __name(...) helper calls that break inside Playwright's page.evaluate serialization → post_join_setup_error. Added __name polyfill via context.addInitScript for hot-dev. Production tsc path doesn't emit __name so the polyfill is a no-op there
meeting-10 listener: diarizer ready, admitted, post-admission OK, audio+caption routing active. Awaiting user audio in meeting

meeting-13 listener (test@vexa.ai user_id=2): pyannote+wespeaker loaded from baked cache; diarizer committed 4× speaker_0; whisper=3 (209ms avg); 1 confirmed segment: 'But it also protects the population because these companies...'. confirm_latency=4.3s. Browser-side audioQueue gating was BLOCKING us — fixed by recording.ts surgery (next op).

deleted audioQueue + speaker-change-flush + text-growth-flush in services/vexa-bot/core/src/platforms/msteams/recording.ts (~80 lines). Browser-side onaudioprocess now dispatches every frame straight to __vexaTeamsAudioData; captions only feed __vexaTeamsCaptionData for the attributor. Banner changed to 'Audio routing active (diarizer-driven, no caption gate)'.

gateway returned 403 'Insufficient scope' for the bot-only token (scopes={bot}); harness-created token (scopes={bot,tx,browser}) returns transcripts correctly. Dashboard sign-in needs the full-scope token.

single-speaker live meetings have no pyannote boundary events, so default maxUtteranceMs=10000 caused 10s commit cadence (felt 'broken' on dashboard). Tuned to maxUtteranceMs=3000 for live; pack synthetic eval re-runs use the default.

original gate (RMS>=0.012, >=600ms speech, >=50% speech_ratio) tuned for clean TTS in RnD eval; silently dropped ALL real Teams audio. Loosened to MIN_TOTAL_SAMPLES>=200ms only. Whisper confidence gates (no_speech_prob/avg_logprob/compression_ratio) remain as tier-2 defence.

meeting-16 produced 1 confirmed segment ('It's very long, but he's talking about in the...' 16.24s) but speakerManager STUCK after — telemetry whisper=1 drafts=1 confirmed=1 frozen for 60+ seconds while diarizer keeps committing every 3s. Likely the discrete commit pattern isn't compatible with speakerManager's continuous-stream session lifecycle. Needs investigation; pack's diarizer+attributor is sound, the audio sink is the bottleneck.

speakerManager confirmed-stuck-after-first-segment was NOT a speakerManager bug. Root cause is in our pack's onCommit drain loop in index.ts: ev.tStartMs/tEndMs are diarizer-session-relative ms (0, 3070, 6140, ...) but teamsPendingFrames[i].ts is Date.now() (wall-clock ~1.7e12). After commit 1 by coincidence drained everything (wall-clock > 3070 always TRUE → break, but other commit-1 frames had been previously consumed), commits 2+ found no frames matching their (session-relative) [tStartMs, tEndMs] window. feedAudio never called → speakerManager idle-trips → emits 1 segment and resets

replaced timestamp-window drain with FIFO drain of wantSamples = round((tEndMs-tStartMs)/1000 * 16000) samples from teamsPendingFrames. The diarizer commits utterances in order so frames for utterance N are the oldest in the buffer. Hot-mount picked it up, no rebuild.

meeting-22: whisper=29, drafts=29, confirmed=13, confirm_latency=4.5s, 9+ confirmed segments streaming to dashboard in real time. Cluster speaker_0 stable. Single-speaker live MS Teams scenario now works end-to-end via the pack's diarizer-driven path
